CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to()
RETURNS TRIGGER
AS $$
BEGIN
  WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT
      ta.id,
      LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(ta.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::date) OVER (
        PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
        ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(ta.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), ta.id
      ) AS next_date
    FROM team_attributes ta
    WHERE ta.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
  )
  UPDATE team_attributes t
  SET eff_to = COALESCE(o.next_date - 1, DATE '5999-12-31')
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE t.id = o.id;

  RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    LEAST(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS start_ts,
    GREATEST(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS end_ts
),
days AS (
  SELECT
    d::timestamp AS day_start,
    (d + interval '1 day') AS day_end
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
    date_trunc('day', p.start_ts),
    date_trunc('day', p.end_ts),
    interval '1 day'
  ) AS d
  WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- weekdays only (Mon=1 .. Fri=5)
),
overlaps AS (
  SELECT
    GREATEST(p.start_ts, day_start) AS seg_start,
    LEAST(p.end_ts, day_end) AS seg_end
  FROM days
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
sum_seconds AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM seg_end - seg_start)), 0) AS total_seconds
  FROM overlaps
  WHERE seg_end > seg_start
),
parts AS (
  SELECT
    FLOOR(total_seconds / 86400)::bigint AS days,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 86400) / 3600)::bigint AS hours,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 3600) / 60)::bigint AS minutes,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 60))::bigint AS seconds
  FROM sum_seconds
)
SELECT
  days || ' days ' || hours || ' hours ' || minutes || ' minutes ' || seconds || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM parts;

WITH
-- 1) One row per item with a clean retail price (ignore NaN/infinite)
items AS (
  SELECT
    s.item_id,
    MIN(CASE WHEN isfinite(s.item_price) THEN s.item_price END) AS retail_price
  FROM store s
  GROUP BY s.item_id
),

-- 2) Direct resource-only craft cost (partial craft price)
partial AS (
  SELECT
    s.item_id,
    COALESCE(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND isfinite(s.resource_price)
            THEN s.resource_price * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0)
        END
      ),
      0
    ) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM store s
  GROUP BY s.item_id
),

-- 3) Expand the bill-of-materials recursively over item dependencies
--    mult = product of quantities along the dependency path
bom AS (
  SELECT
    i.item_id AS root_item,
    i.item_id AS current_item,
    1::numeric AS mult,
    ARRAY[i.item_id]::int[] AS path
  FROM items i

  UNION ALL

  SELECT
    b.root_item,
    s.uses_item_id AS current_item,
    b.mult * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0),
    b.path || s.uses_item_id
  FROM bom b
  JOIN store s
    ON s.item_id = b.current_item
  WHERE s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
    -- avoid cycles if any exist
    AND NOT (s.uses_item_id = ANY (b.path))
),

-- 4) Full craft price = sum of all resources needed across the entire dependency tree
full_cost AS (
  SELECT
    b.root_item AS item_id,
    COALESCE(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND isfinite(s.resource_price)
            THEN s.resource_price * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0) * b.mult
        END
      ),
      0
    ) AS full_craft_price
  FROM bom b
  JOIN store s
    ON s.item_id = b.current_item
  GROUP BY b.root_item
)

SELECT
  i.item_id,
  i.retail_price,
  p.partial_craft_price,
  f.full_craft_price,
  -- Optional: which is cheaper overall (buy vs craft)
  CASE
    WHEN i.retail_price IS NULL THEN 'CRAFT'
    WHEN f.full_craft_price IS NULL THEN 'BUY'
    WHEN f.full_craft_price < i.retail_price THEN 'CRAFT'
    ELSE 'BUY'
  END AS cheaper_option
FROM items i
LEFT JOIN partial p ON p.item_id = i.item_id
LEFT JOIN full_cost f ON f.item_id = i.item_id
ORDER BY i.item_id;

WITH buys AS (
  SELECT
    t.molecule_id,
    t.date AS buy_date,
    t.transaction_id AS buy_txn_id,
    t.molecules AS buy_qty,
    t.usd_total AS buy_total,
    (t.usd_total / t.molecules::numeric) AS buy_unit_price,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) - t.molecules AS buy_cum_start,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) AS buy_cum_end
  FROM "transaction" t
  WHERE t.sell = false
),
sells AS (
  SELECT
    t.molecule_id,
    t.date AS sell_date,
    t.transaction_id AS sell_txn_id,
    t.molecules AS sell_qty,
    t.usd_total AS sell_total,
    (t.usd_total / t.molecules::numeric) AS sell_unit_price,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) - t.molecules AS sell_cum_start,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) AS sell_cum_end
  FROM "transaction" t
  WHERE t.sell = true
),
matches AS (
  SELECT
    b.molecule_id,
    b.buy_date,
    s.sell_date,
    GREATEST(
      0,
      LEAST(b.buy_cum_end, s.sell_cum_end) - GREATEST(b.buy_cum_start, s.sell_cum_start)
    )::integer AS matched_qty,
    b.buy_unit_price,
    s.sell_unit_price
  FROM buys b
  JOIN sells s
    ON s.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
   AND (
         s.sell_date > b.buy_date
      OR (s.sell_date = b.buy_date AND s.sell_txn_id > b.buy_txn_id)
   )
  WHERE LEAST(b.buy_cum_end, s.sell_cum_end) > GREATEST(b.buy_cum_start, s.sell_cum_start)
)
SELECT
  molecule_id,
  buy_date,
  sell_date,
  matched_qty AS molecules,
  (matched_qty * buy_unit_price) AS buy_usd_total,
  (matched_qty * sell_unit_price) AS sell_usd_total
FROM matches
WHERE matched_qty > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, buy_date, sell_date;

WITH RECURSIVE lineage AS (
  -- Start at every set that has a direct parent
  SELECT
    s.id AS start_id,
    s.code AS start_code,
    s.parentcode AS next_code,
    (s.code || '-' || s.parentcode) AS path,
    ARRAY[s.code] AS visited
  FROM sets s
  WHERE s.parentcode IS NOT NULL

  UNION ALL

  -- Walk up the chain, appending each ancestor code
  SELECT
    l.start_id,
    l.start_code,
    p.parentcode AS next_code,
    l.path || CASE WHEN p.parentcode IS NOT NULL THEN '-' || p.parentcode ELSE '' END AS path,
    CASE WHEN p.code IS NOT NULL THEN l.visited || p.code ELSE l.visited END AS visited
  FROM lineage l
  LEFT JOIN sets p
    ON p.code = l.next_code
  -- prevent cycles; allow termination when parent row is missing
  WHERE p.code IS NULL OR NOT (p.code = ANY(l.visited))
)
SELECT
  start_id AS id,
  start_code AS code,
  path AS ancestralCode
FROM lineage
WHERE next_code IS NULL
ORDER BY code;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT 10::int AS cap, 7::int AS max_items
),
my_table AS (
  SELECT 'A'::text AS cto, 1::int AS nif, 'A1'::text AS file_, 5::int AS filesize
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 1, 'A2', 3
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A3', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A4', 1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B1', 4
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B2', 6
),
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    t.*,
    row_number() OVER (
      PARTITION BY cto
      ORDER BY substring(file_ FROM 2)::int, file_
    ) AS rn,
    lag(nif) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cto
      ORDER BY substring(file_ FROM 2)::int, file_
    ) AS prev_nif
  FROM my_table t
),
rows_with_block AS (
  SELECT
    o.*,
    /* consecutive block id: increments only when nif changes within cto */
    sum(CASE WHEN prev_nif IS DISTINCT FROM nif THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY rn ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS block_id
  FROM ordered o
),
blocks AS (
  /* One row per (cto, block) with its total size and natural order */
  SELECT
    cto,
    block_id,
    min(rn) AS block_first_rn,
    sum(filesize)::int AS block_size
  FROM rows_with_block
  GROUP BY cto, block_id
),
blocks_with_running AS (
  SELECT
    b.*,
    /* cumulative size up to and including this block */
    sum(block_size) OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY block_id
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cum_size
  FROM blocks b
),
mn_groups AS (
  /* First-level grouping (subgroups): add entire next block only if cap won't be exceeded.
     Using: group_id = floor((cum_with_current - 1) / cap) + 1 */
  SELECT
    bw.cto,
    bw.block_id,
    bw.block_size,
    bw.block_first_rn,
    ((bw.cum_size - 1) / p.cap) + 1 AS mn
  FROM blocks_with_running bw
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
final_groups AS (
  /* Second-level grouping: limit to max_items subgroups per final partition */
  SELECT
    g.*,
    ((g.mn - 1) / p.max_items) + 1 AS partition,
    ((g.mn - 1) % p.max_items) + 1 AS subpartition
  FROM mn_groups g
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
rows_tagged AS (
  SELECT
    r.cto,
    r.nif,
    r.file_,
    fg.partition,
    fg.subpartition
  FROM rows_with_block r
  JOIN final_groups fg
    ON fg.cto = r.cto
   AND fg.block_id = r.block_id
)
SELECT
  rt.cto,
  rt.nif,
  rt.file_,
  rt.partition,
  rt.subpartition,
  /* total size of all files in the final (cto, partition) */
  sum(t.filesize) OVER (PARTITION BY rt.cto, rt.partition)::int AS total_size
FROM rows_tagged rt
JOIN my_table t
  ON t.cto = rt.cto
 AND t.file_ = rt.file_
ORDER BY
  rt.cto,
  substring(rt.file_ FROM 2)::int,
  rt.file_;
